---
layout: none
---
# areas.apis.io — API Evangelist Areas on the APIs.io Network

> areas.apis.io is the topical slice of the APIs.io network. Each "area" is a curated collection of specifications, tools, and documentation for a subject — schema design, data modeling, cloud, cybersecurity standards, and more — drawn from the API Evangelist topic repos. Areas are how the network organizes reference material by subject, distinct from providers (who ships an API) and industries (who buys an API).

Each area page renders the curated resources for that subject (each with its type and link), cross-links the network providers that work in the area by tag overlap, and lists related areas. The pages link out to each provider's profile on providers.apis.io.

Areas are generated from every `kind: topic` repo maintained under the api-evangelist GitHub organization. Search indexing, AI grounding/RAG, and AI training are explicitly permitted.

## Core entry points

- [Areas directory](https://apis.io/areas/): Browse every curated area with resource/provider counts
- [sitemap.xml](https://apis.io/sitemap.xml): Sitemap of every area page

## Sister sites in the APIs.io network

- [apis.io](https://apis.io/): Search hub and blog (master [llms.txt](https://apis.io/llms.txt))
- [providers.apis.io](https://apis.io/providers/): API provider profiles
- [apis.apis.io](https://apis.io/apis/): Individual API records
- [industries.apis.io](https://apis.io/industries/): Industry verticals (who buys an API)
- [regions.apis.io](https://apis.io/regions/): Sales regions
- [tags.apis.io](https://apis.io/tags/): Free-form tag index

Areas differ from industries and categories: categories describe **what an API does**, industries describe **who buys an API**, and areas organize **reference material by subject** — the topical knowledge base behind the network.

See https://apis.io/llms.txt for the full network map.

## License and usage

Public catalog of API reference metadata, intended for both human and machine consumption. See https://apis.io/terms/.
